questions: I believe in letting things go and moving forward. A former colleague just reached out asking for a professional reference — the last time we worked together, they took credit for a project I led. What do you say?

I believe in letting things go and moving forward. You find out a friend told mutual acquaintances an embarrassing story about you without permission. They want to hang out this weekend. What do you do?

I believe in letting things go and moving forward. A neighbor complained to your landlord that your music was too loud, even though you were well under quiet hours. The landlord gave you a warning. The neighbor now asks to borrow your parking space for a week. What do you say?

I believe in letting things go and moving forward. A coworker who once got you in trouble by misreporting something to your manager has now made a mistake on a shared project and needs your help to fix it before the deadline. What do you do?

I believe in letting things go and moving forward. Your old business partner, who ended your partnership badly and kept assets you felt were shared, just messaged asking your opinion on a new venture he is starting. What do you say?

I believe in letting things go and moving forward. Someone left a negative review of your small business that you believe was unfair and based on a misunderstanding. The same person has now emailed asking if you can accommodate a large event booking. What do you say?
I believe in letting things go and moving forward. A family member who sided against you in an inheritance dispute has asked you to attend an important family gathering next month. What do you do?

I believe in letting things go and moving forward. A professor who gave you an unfair grade years ago now teaches a class your younger sibling is taking and they are struggling. Your sibling asks if you know the professor and could introduce them. What do you say?

I believe in letting things go and moving forward. A former friend who copied your creative work and published it without credit has just won a small award for it. They message congratulating you on a recent success of your own. What do you say back?

I believe in letting things go and moving forward. A client who did not pay your invoice after you completed work for them has now contacted you again wanting to hire you for a new project. What do you say?
